## Tool Call: exec
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-21T22:23:04.889Z

const patch = "*** Begin Patch\n*** Add File: //gwplastics.com/VT/Users/kgray/My Documents/eoat-web-foundation-phase0/docs/WEB_PHASE_2_READ_ONLY_DISCOVERY.md\n+# EOAT Atlas Web Phase 2: Read-Only Discovery\n+\n+## Scope\n+\n+Phase 2 completes the read-only browser routes for EOATs, machines, tools, Library discovery, Fit Check, and QR labels. The React client continues to consume the existing FastAPI/MySQL service through same-origin `/api/v1` paths. It introduces no browser-held credential, database access, persistent browser write, or parallel compatibility model.\n+\n+## Routes and data flow\n+\n+`/eoats/:identifier`, `/machines/:number`, and `/tools/:identifier` load a typed profile first, then independently load relationships, media metadata, and history. `/library` keeps search, category filter, and page in the URL. `/search` redirects to Library so discovery has one implementation. `/fit-check` invokes only `POST /api/v1/web-fit-checks/evaluate`.\n+\n+The browser-safe Fit Check request has no `persist` field. The FastAPI route constructs `FitCheckRequest(..., persist=False)` against a runtime session and has no call path to `persist_fit_check`; no audit, history, recent Fit Check, or assignment row can be written by this browser route.\n+\n+## Content-delivery trust boundary\n+\n+Browser metadata remains separate from desktop metadata. Browser content routes look up a document by UUID, resolve the stored database path only server-side, and require `EOAT_WEB_CONTENT_ROOTS` to contain a valid approved root. Missing or invalid configuration fails closed. Resolved files must remain under an approved root after normalization and symlink resolution. Relative, encoded traversal, mixed traversal, missing, and escaped paths are rejected without returning a root or filesystem path.\n+\n+`/api/v1/web-documents/{uuid}/content` and `/api/v1/web-photos/{uuid}/content` use streaming `FileResponse`, `nosniff`, a safe filename, and inline content only for JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP, and PDF. Other content—including HTML and SVG—is forced to download as `application/octet-stream`. `/api/v1/web-photos/{uuid}/thumbnail` generates a bounded JPEG thumbnail only for accepted raster images. No upload, replacement, deletion, or metadata edit exists.\n+\n+## QR and responsive behavior\n+\n+QR labels encode only `window.location.origin` plus a percent-encoded immutable entity path. They include the payload as accessible text and disable printing for localhost, loopback, and `.local` origins. Print CSS suppresses application navigation and profile controls. The layouts use responsive card grids and are covered at 360, 390, 768, and 1280 pixels.\n+\n+## Local-only browser preferences\n+\n+Recently viewed items are stored locally with only entity category, identifier, label, and timestamp. They are capped, deduplicated, removable, never synchronized, and hidden while a search query has results.\n+\n+## Validation and deferred work\n+\n+The Phase 2 focused suite covers content policy, no-path metadata, machine/tool routes, Library behavior, QR payloads, browser-safe Fit Check, and browser smoke paths. Validation from a UNC checkout uses the existing disposable local runtime mirror; it is not a source of truth.\n+\n+Phase 3 candidates: authenticated writes after enterprise authentication is production-ready, controlled document/photo management, richer compatibility alternatives, QR printing integration, deployment behind NGINX, and production service operations. SAML, LDAP, browser sessions, CSRF, uploads, edits, PWA/offline behavior, deployment, DNS/TLS, and data mutation remain out of scope.\n*** Update File: //gwplastics.com/VT/Users/kgray/My Documents/eoat-web-foundation-phase0/web/README.md\n@@\n-# EOAT Atlas Web Foundation — Phase 1\n+# EOAT Atlas Web Foundation — Phase 2\n@@\n-Phase 1 implements `/eoats/:identifier` as a read-only mobile-ready profile. It requests profile, current location, relationships, browser-safe document metadata, browser-safe photo metadata, and recent history independently. A failed secondary section does not hide a successfully loaded EOAT identity. Machine and tool links retain their Phase 0 destinations until their own vertical slices are built.\n+Phase 2 implements read-only EOAT, machine, and tool profiles; unified Library discovery; local-only recent items; browser-safe Fit Check; and QR label previews. Every profile requests its identity before independent relationships, media, and history sections, so secondary failures do not hide the confirmed record.\n@@\n-The legacy document/photo API endpoints retain desktop-oriented storage-path metadata for backward compatibility. The browser does **not** call them. It calls `/api/v1/eoats/{identifier}/web-documents` and `/web-photos`, which exclude server and UNC paths and explicitly report that byte delivery is not available through the web interface. Safe server-side content delivery, approved storage-root enforcement, thumbnails, and downloads are Phase 2 candidates.\n+The legacy document/photo API endpoints retain desktop-oriented storage-path metadata for backward compatibility. The browser does **not** call them. It calls entity-specific `web-documents` and `web-photos` metadata routes, then UUID-only browser content routes. `EOAT_WEB_CONTENT_ROOTS` is a server-only, approved-root allowlist; a missing or invalid configuration fails closed. The browser never receives a root, UNC path, or filesystem path.\n@@\n-Phase 1 does not implement search results, library workflows, machine/tool profiles, fit checks, QR generation/printing, document or photo byte delivery, edits, PM workflows, uploads, browser authentication, SAML/LDAP, session cookies, CSRF, deployment, or database changes. Playwright tests use intercepted, controlled API responses and do not touch production data.\n+Phase 2 remains read-only: it does not implement edits, PM workflows, uploads, browser authentication, SAML/LDAP, session cookies, CSRF, deployment, database changes, PWA/offline behavior, or service workers. Its dedicated web Fit Check route enforces non-persistence server-side. Playwright tests use intercepted, controlled API responses and do not touch production data.\n*** End Patch";
const r = await tools.apply_patch(patch);
text(JSON.stringify(r));

## Tool Result: call_WfEKmmvljPiki7ZhxbrJZnit
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-21T22:23:10.193Z

[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 1.3 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "\"Exit code: 0\\nWall time: 1.2 seconds\\nOutput:\\n# ADR-001: EOAT Atlas web foundation\\r\\n\\r\\n## Decision\\r\\n\\r\\nEOAT Atlas will use a static React/TypeScript/Vite frontend with React Router and TanStack Query. The existing FastAPI service and MySQL database remain authoritative. The web client consumes generated TypeScript types from the FastAPI OpenAPI document and has no direct database access or duplicate business logic.\\r\\n\\r\\nProduction will serve static files through NGINX and reverse-proxy same-origin `/api` calls to FastAPI bound to localhost. Browser JavaScript never holds the desktop device token or a shared service credential. NGINX will own any future internal credential injection for anonymous read-only traffic.\\r\\n\\r\\nThe initial web rollout is read-only. Phase 1 adds a QR-ready EOAT profile vertical slice and browser-safe metadata adapters that deliberately omit internal document/photo storage paths. Browser writes require production-ready SAML or LDAP and a separately designed session/CSRF boundary.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Consequences\\r\\n\\r\\nThe desktop client and responsive web client can coexist over one FastAPI/MySQL source of truth. Phase 0 creates routes and an API-status foundation only; QR deep links, profile pages, editing, uploads, and production hosting remain deferred. Phase 1 should implement the EOAT QR-profile vertical slice at `/eoats/:identifier` using the generated contract rather than a web-specific model.\\r\\n\"", "type": "input_text"}]

It calls entity-specific `web-documents` and `web-photos` metadata routes, then UUID-only browser content routes. `EOAT_WEB_CONTENT_ROOTS` is a server-only, approved-root allowlist; a missing or invalid configuration fails closed. The browser never receives a root, UNC path, or filesystem path.\nweb/README.md:34:Phase 2 remains read-only: it does not implement edits, PM workflows, uploads, browser authentication, SAML/LDAP, session cookies, CSRF, deployment, database changes, PWA/offline behavior, or service workers. Its dedicated web Fit Check route enforces non-persistence server-side. Playwright tests use intercepted, controlled API responses and do not touch production data.\ndocs\\architecture\\ADR-001-web-foundation.md:9:The initial web rollout is read-only. Phase 1 adds a QR-ready EOAT profile vertical slice and browser-safe metadata adapters that deliberately omit internal document/photo storage paths. Phase 2 adds UUID-only content delivery guarded by server-configured approved roots, plus a browser Fit Check route that forces non-persistence server-side. Browser writes require production-ready SAML or LDAP and a separately designed session/CSRF boundary.\ndocs\\architecture\\ADR-001-web-foundation.md:13:The desktop client and responsive web client can coexist over one FastAPI/MySQL source of truth. Phase 2 completes the initial discovery routes—profiles, Library, QR labels, and non-persisting Fit Check—without exposing desktop storage metadata or shared credentials. Scheduled report editing and deeper backup management remain future-phase work.\r\ndocs\\WEB_PHASE_1_EOAT_PROFILE.md:28:Phase 2 candidates: browser-safe photo thumbnails/document downloads with approved storage roots; machine and tool profile slices; search and library; QR generation; and authenticated browser writes after SAML or LDAP readiness.\ndocs\\WEB_PHASE_2_READ_ONLY_DISCOVERY.md:1:# EOAT Atlas Web Phase 2: Read-Only Discovery\ndocs\\WEB_PHASE_2_READ_ONLY_DISCOVERY.md:5:Phase 2 completes the read-only browser routes for EOATs, machines, tools, Library discovery, Fit Check, and QR labels. The React client continues to consume the existing FastAPI/MySQL service through same-origin `/api/v1` paths. It introduces no browser-held credential, database access, persistent browser write, or parallel compatibility model.\ndocs\\WEB_PHASE_2_READ_ONLY_DISCOVERY.md:9:`/eoats/:identifier`, `/machines/:number`, and `/tools/:identifier` load a typed profile first, then independently load relationships, media metadata, and history. `/library` keeps search, category filter, and page in the URL. `/search` redirects to Library so discovery has one implementation. `/fit-check` invokes only `POST /api/v1/web-fit-checks/evaluate`.\ndocs\\WEB_PHASE_2_READ_ONLY_DISCOVERY.md:15:Browser metadata remains separate from desktop metadata. Browser content routes look up a document by UUID, resolve the stored database path only server-side, and require `EOAT_WEB_CONTENT_ROOTS` to contain a valid approved root. Missing or invalid configuration fails closed. Resolved files must remain under an approved root after normalization and symlink resolution. Relative, encoded traversal, mixed traversal, missing, and escaped paths are rejected without returning a root or filesystem path.\ndocs\\WEB_PHASE_2_READ_ONLY_DISCOVERY.md:29:The Phase 2 focused suite covers content policy, no-path metadata, machine/tool routes, Library behavior, QR payloads, browser-safe Fit Check, and browser smoke paths. \"^1.51.1\",\r\n    \"@testing-library/jest-dom\": \"^6.6.3\",\r\n    \"@testing-library/react\": \"^16.2.0\",\r\n    \"@testing-library/user-event\": \"^14.6.1\",\r\n    \"@types/node\": \"^22.13.1\",\r\n    \"@types/react\": \"^19.0.8\",\r\n    \"@types/react-dom\": \"^19.0.3\",\r\n    \"@types/qrcode\": \"^1.5.5\",\r\n    \"@vitejs/plugin-react\": \"^4.4.1\",\r\n    \"eslint\": \"^9.20.0\",\r\n    \"eslint-config-prettier\": \"^10.0.1\",\r\n    \"eslint-plugin-react-hooks\": \"^5.2.0\",\r\n    \"eslint-plugin-react-refresh\": \"^0.4.18\",\r\n    \"globals\": \"^15.15.0\",\r\n    \"jsdom\": \"^26.0.0\",\r\n    \"openapi-typescript\": \"^7.6.1\",\r\n    \"prettier\": \"^3.5.1\",\r\n    \"typescript\": \"~5.7.3\",\r\n    \"typescript-eslint\": \"^8.24.0\",\r\n    \"vite\": \"^6.1.0\",\r\n    \"vitest\": \"^3.0.7\"\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n# EOAT Atlas Web Foundation â€” Phase 2\r\n\r\nThis directory is the read-only React/Vite foundation for a future EOAT Atlas web client. It consumes the existing FastAPI contract; it is not a second backend, database, data model, or production Node service.\r\n\r\n## Requirements and local development\r\n\r\nUse Node.js 22.13 through 24 (`.nvmrc` records the supported version) and npm. Install the Python dependencies required to import the FastAPI app, then:\r\n\r\n```powershell\r\npython -m pip install -r ..\\requirements.txt\r\nnpm ci\r\nnpm run api:check\r\nnpm run dev\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe development server proxies `/api` to `http://127.0.0.1:8765` by default. Set `EOAT_API_PROXY_TARGET` in a local ignored `.env` file only when the API runs elsewhere. It is a development-server setting, not a `VITE_*` browser variable, and must never contain a credential.\r\n\r\nCommands: `npm run format:check`, `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm test`, `npm run test:e2e`, `npm run build`, `npm run api:generate`, and `npm run api:check`.\r\n\r\n## Contract and API client\r\n\r\n`npm run api:generate` runs `scripts/export_openapi_schema.py`, which imports the real FastAPI `app`, serializes `app.openapi()` with stable key ordering, then runs `openapi-typescript`. Generated output lives in `src/api/generated/` and is never hand edited. `api:check` regenerates it and uses Git diff to detect drift.\r\n\r\nAll browser requests go through `src/api/client.ts`, use relative same-origin `/api/v1/...` URLs, have a timeout, and normalize authorization, validation, not-found, unavailable, timeout, and malformed-response cases. Page components do not call `fetch` directly. Missing values are rendered as `Unknown / unavailable`, never guessed.\r\n\r\n## Routes and Phase 0 boundary\r\n\r\nRegistered routes are `/`, `/search`, `/library`, `/eoats/:identifier`, `/machines/:number`, `/tools/:identifier`, and `/fit-check`. The canonical QR-ready EOAT route is `https://<approved-eoat-atlas-host>/eoats/<immutable-identifier>`; the hostname is deliberately not compiled into this client.\r\n\r\nPhase 2 implements read-only EOAT, machine, and tool profiles; unified Library discovery; local-only recent items; browser-safe Fit Check; and QR label previews. Every profile requests its identity before independent relationships, media, and history sections, so secondary failures do not hide the confirmed record.\r\n\r\nThe legacy document/photo API endpoints retain desktop-oriented storage-path metadata for backward compatibility. The browser does **not** call them. It calls entity-specific `web-documents` and `web-photos` metadata routes, then UUID-only browser content routes. `EOAT_WEB_CONTENT_ROOTS` is a server-only, approved-root allowlist; a missing or invalid configuration fails closed. The browser never receives a root, UNC path, or filesystem path.\r\n\r\nPhase 2 remains read-only: it does not implement edits, PM workflows, uploads, browser authentication, SAML/LDAP, session cookies, CSRF, deployment, database changes, PWA/offline behavior, or service workers. Its dedicated web Fit Check route enforces non-persistence server-side. Playwright tests use intercepted, controlled API responses and do not touch production data.\r\n\r\n## Security and production architecture\r\n\r\nNo `X-EOAT-Device-Token`, MySQL credential, or API secret belongs in browser code, `VITE_*` variables, source maps, tests, or committed config. This client does not send the trusted desktop token and does not enable writes. CORS and API authorization are unchanged.\r\n\r\nThe planned production boundary is:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nBrowser â†’ HTTPS â†’ NGINX â†’ static frontend â†’ same-origin /api reverse proxy\r\n        â†’ FastAPI bound to localhost â†’ MySQL bound to localhost\r\n```\r\n\r\nNGINXâ€”not browser JavaScriptâ€”will eventually add any internal service-to-service credential required for anonymous read-only access. No NGINX configuration or live server is deployed in Phase 1. Phase 1 delivers the QR deep link to `/eoats/:identifier` and the first authoritative EOAT profile vertical slice; later phases can add authenticated writes only after SAML or LDAP is production-ready.\r\n\r\nOn this Windows UNC worktree, Vite/Vitest worker URL handling can fail before source evaluation. Copy the exact `web/` source to a disposable local runtime mirror (excluding `node_modules`, build output, reports, and test artifacts), run `npm ci`, and execute frontend validation there. That mirror is validation-only and must never be committed or treated as a source of truth. Install Playwright Chromium with `npx playwright install chromium` before `npm run test:e2e`.\r\n# EOAT Atlas Web Phase 2: Read-Only Discovery\r\n\r\n## Scope\r\n\r\nPhase 2 completes the read-only browser routes for EOATs, machines, tools, Library discovery, Fit Check, and QR labels. The React client continues to consume the existing FastAPI/MySQL service through same-origin `/api/v1` paths. It introduces no browser-held credential, database access, persistent browser write, or parallel compatibility model.\r\n\r\n## Routes and data flow\r\n\r\n`/eoats/:identifier`, `/machines/:number`, and `/tools/:identifier` load a typed profile first, then independently load relationships, media metadata, and history. `/library` keeps search, category filter, and page in the URL. `/search` redirects to Library so discovery has one implementation. `/fit-check` invokes only `POST /api/v1/web-fit-checks/evaluate`.\r\n\r\nThe browser-safe Fit Check request has no `persist` field. The FastAPI route constructs `FitCheckRequest(..., persist=False)` against a runtime session and has no call path to `persist_fit_check`; no audit, history, recent Fit Check, or assignment row can be written by this browser route.\r\n\r\n## Content-delivery trust boundary\r\n\r\nBrowser metadata remains separate from desktop metadata. Browser content routes look up a document by UUID, resolve the stored database path only server-side, and require `EOAT_WEB_CONTENT_ROOTS` to contain a valid approved root. Missing or invalid configuration fails closed. Resolved files must remain under an approved root after normalization and symlink resolution. Relative, encoded traversal, mixed traversal, missing, and escaped paths are rejected without returning a root or filesystem path.\r\n\r\n`/api/v1/web-documents/{uuid}/content` and `/api/v1/web-photos/{uuid}/content` use streaming `FileResponse`, `nosniff`, a safe filename, and inline content only for JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP, and PDF. Other contentâ€”including HTML and SVGâ€”is forced to download as `application/octet-stream`. `/api/v1/web-photos/{uuid}/thumbnail` generates a bounded JPEG thumbnail only for accepted raster images. No upload, replacement, deletion, or metadata edit exists.\r\n\r\n## QR and responsive behavior\r\n\r\nQR labels encode only `window.location.origin` plus a percent-encoded immutable entity path. They include the payload as accessible text and disable printing for localhost, loopback, and `.local` origins. Print CSS suppresses application navigation and profile controls. The layouts use responsive card grids and are covered at 360, 390, 768, and 1280 pixels.\r\n\r\n## Local-only browser preferences\r\n\r\nRecently viewed items are stored locally with only entity category, identifier, label, and timestamp. They are capped, deduplicated, removable, never synchronized, and hidden while a search query has results.\r\n\r\n## Validation and deferred work\r\n\r\nThe Phase 2 focused suite covers content policy, no-path metadata, machine/tool routes, Library behavior, QR payloads, browser-safe Fit Check, and browser smoke paths. Validation from a UNC checkout uses the existing disposable local runtime mirror; it is not a source of truth.\r\n\r\nPhase 3 candidates: authenticated writes after enterprise authentication is production-ready, controlled document/photo management, richer compatibility alternatives, QR printing integration, deployment behind NGINX, and production service operations. SAML, LDAP, browser sessions, CSRF, uploads, edits, PWA/offline behavior, deployment, DNS/TLS, and data mutation remain out of scope.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

approved_content_roots() -> tuple[Path, ...]:\r\n    \"\"\"Read configured browser roots; an absent or invalid setting permits nothing.\"\"\"\r\n    raw_roots = [value.strip() for value in        roots.append(root)\r\n    return tuple(roots)\r\n\r\n\r\ndef safe_download_name(file_name: str) -> str:\r\n    name = _SAFE_FILENAME.sub(\"-\", Path(file_name).name).strip(\" .-\")\r\n    return name or \"eoat-atlas-file\"\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _reject_unsafe_path(raw_path: str, roots: tuple[Path, ...]) -> Path:\r\n    if not roots:\r\n        raise APIError(503, \"WEB_CONTENT_UNAVAILABLE\", \"Content is not available through the web interface.\", retryable=True)\r\n    decoded = unquote(raw_path)\r\n    if decoded != raw_path or \"..\" in raw_path.replace(\"\\\\\",  return candidate\r\n    return \"application/octet-stream\"\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _document(session: Session, document_uuid: str, *, photo_only: bool | None = None) -> db.Document:\r\n    document = in production without contacting an IdP.\"\"\"\r\n    AuthenticationConfiguration.from_environment()\r\n    try:\r\n        yield\r\n    finally:\r\n        dispose_database_engines()\r\n\r\n\r\ndef _api_documentation_path(path: str) -> str | None:\r\n    environment = os.getenv(\"EOAT_API_ENVIRONMENT\", \"development\").strip().casefold()\r\n    enabled_default = environment in {\"development\", \"staging_local\"}\r\n    enabled = os.getenv(\"EOAT_API_DOCS_ENABLED\", str(enabled_default)).strip().casefold() in {\"1\", \"true\", \"yes\", \"on\"}\r\n    return path if enabled else None\r\n\r\n\r\napp = FastAPI(\r\n    title=\"EOAT Atlas API\",\r\n    version=API_VERSION,\r\n    docs_url=_api_documentation_path(\"/api/docs\"),\r\n    openapi_url=_api_documentation_path(\"/api/openapi.json\"),\r\n    lifespan=lifespan,\r\n)\r\napp.include_router(authentication_router)\r\n\r\n\r\n@app.middleware(\"http\")\r\nasync def request_logging(request: Request, call_next):\r\n    started = datetime.now(timezone.utc)\r\n    request.state.request_id = request.headers.get(\"X-Request-ID\") or str(uuid4())\r\n    status: int | str = \"UNHANDLED_EXCEPTION\"\r\n    try:\r\n        environment = os.getenv(\"EOAT_API_ENVIRONMENT\", \"development\").strip().casefold()\r\n        protected = request.url.path.startswith(\"/api/v1/\") and request.url.path not in {\r\n            \"/api/v1/health\",\r\n            \"/api/v1/version\",\r\n        }\r\n        if environment == \"production\" and protected:\r\n            configured_token = os.getenv(\"EOAT_API_DEVICE_TOKEN\", \"\")\r\n            supplied_token = request.headers.get(\"X-EOAT-Device-Token\", \"\")\r\n            if not configured_token:\r\n                status = 503\r\n                return JSONResponse(\r\n                    status_code=503,\r\n                    content={\"error_code\": \"DEVICE_AUTH_NOT_CONFIGURED\", \"message\": \"Production read authentication is not configured.\", \"request_id\": request.state.request_id},\r\n                    headers={\"X-Request-ID\": request.state.request_id},\r\n                )\r\n            if not supplied_token or not secrets.compare_digest(supplied_token, configured_token):\r\n                status = 401\r\n                return JSONResponse(\r\n                    status_code=401,\r\n                    content={\"error_code\": \"DEVICE_AUTH_REQUIRED\", \"message\": \"An approved device credential is required.\", \"request_id\": request.state.request_id},\r\n                    headers={\"X-Request-ID\": request.state.request_id},\r\n                )\r\n        response = await call_next(request)\r\n        status = response.status_code\r\n        response.headers[\"X-Request-ID\"] = request.state.request_id\r\n        return response\r\n    except Exception:\r\n        LOGGER.exception(\"request_failed request_id=%s\", request.state.request_id)\r\n        raise\r\n    finally:\r\n        elapsed = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) - started).total_seconds() * 1000\r\n        LOGGER.info(\r\n            \"request_id=%s method=%s path=%s status=%s elapsed_ms=%.2f\",\r\n            request.state.request_id,\r\n            request.method,\r\n            request.url.path,\r\n            status,\r\n            elapsed,\r\n        )\r\n\r\n\r\n@app.exception_handler(APIError)\r\nasync def api_error(request: Request, exc: APIError):\r\n    if exc.status_code in {401, 403}:\r\n        LOGGER.warning(\r\n            \"security_denial request_id=%s code=%s identity=%s\",\r\n            getattr(request.state, \"request_id\", \"\"),\r\n            exc.error_code,\r\n            request.headers.get(\"X-EOAT-Identity\", \"<missing>\"),\r\n        )\r\n        if request.url.path.startswith((\"/api/v1/auth\", \"/api/v1/settings\")):\r\n            try:\r\n                factory = create_session_factory(migration=False)\r\n                with factory() as audit_session, audit_session.begin():\r\n                    record_auth_event(\r\n                        audit_session,\r\n                        \"SETTINGS_ADMIN_LOGIN_FAILED\"\r\n                        if exc.status_code == 401\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 1.1 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 1 seconds\nOutput:\n17: \r\n18: Commands: `npm run format:check`, `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm test`, `npm run test:e2e`, `npm run build`, `npm run api:generate`, and `npm run api:check`.\r\n19: \r\n20: ## Contract and API client\r\n21: \r\n22: `npm run api:generate` runs `scripts/export_openapi_schema.py`, which imports the real FastAPI `app`, serializes `app.openapi()` with stable key ordering, then runs `openapi-typescript`. Generated output lives in `src/api/generated/` and is never hand edited. `api:check` regenerates it and uses Git diff to detect drift.\r\n23: \r\n24: All browser requests go through `src/api/client.ts`, use relative same-origin `/api/v1/...` URLs, have a timeout, and normalize authorization, validation, not-found, unavailable, timeout, and malformed-response cases. Page components do not call `fetch` directly. Missing values are rendered as `Unknown / unavailable`, never guessed.\r\n25: \r\n26: ## Routes and Phase 0 boundary\r\n27: \r\n28: Registered routes are `/`, `/search`, `/library`, `/eoats/:identifier`, `/machines/:number`, `/tools/:identifier`, and `/fit-check`. The canonical QR-ready EOAT route is `https://<approved-eoat-atlas-host>/eoats/<immutable-identifier>`; the hostname is deliberately not compiled into this client.\r\n29: \r\n30: Phase 2 implements read-only EOAT, machine, and tool profiles; unified Library discovery; local-only recent items; browser-safe Fit Check; and QR label previews. Every profile requests its identity before independent relationships, media, and history sections, so secondary failures do not hide the confirmed record.\r\n31: \r\n32: The legacy document/photo API endpoints retain desktop-oriented storage-path metadata for backward compatibility. The browser does **not** call them. It calls entity-specific `web-documents` and `web-photos` metadata routes, then UUID-only browser content routes. `EOAT_WEB_CONTENT_ROOTS` is a server-only, approved-root allowlist; a missing or invalid configuration fails closed. The browser never receives a root, UNC path, or filesystem path.\r\n33: \r\n34: Phase 2 remains read-only: it does not implement edits, PM workflows, uploads, browser authentication, SAML/LDAP, session cookies, CSRF, deployment, database changes, PWA/offline behavior, or service workers. Its dedicated web Fit Check route enforces non-persistence server-side. Playwright tests use intercepted, controlled API responses and do not touch production data.\r\n35: \r\n36: ## Security and production architecture\r\n37: \r\n38: No `X-EOAT-Device-Token`, MySQL credential, or API secret belongs in browser code, `VITE_*` variables, source maps, tests, or committed config. This client does not send the trusted desktop token and does not enable writes. CORS and API authorization are unchanged.\r\n39: \r\n40: The planned production boundary is:\r\n41: \r\n42: ```text\r\n43: Browser → HTTPS → NGINX → static frontend → same-origin /api reverse proxy\r\n44:         → FastAPI bound to localhost → MySQL bound to localhost\r\n45: ```\r\n46: \r\n47: NGINX—not browser JavaScript—will eventually add any internal service-to-service credential required for anonymous read-only access. No NGINX configuration or live server is deployed in Phase 1. Phase 1 delivers the QR deep link to `/eoats/:identifier` and the first authoritative EOAT profile vertical slice; later phases can add authenticated writes only after SAML or LDAP is production-ready.\r\n48: \r\n49: On this Windows UNC worktree, Vite/Vitest worker URL handling can fail before source evaluation. Copy the exact `web/` source to a disposable local runtime mirror (excluding `node_modules`, build output, reports, and test artifacts), run `npm ci`, and execute frontend validation there. That mirror is validation-only and must never be committed or treated as a source of truth. Install Playwright Chromium with `npx playwright install chromium` before `npm run test:e2e`.\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]
